trail.val: Analyst Day: 10%+ EBITDA & EPS CAGR; contracted base CAGR ~8%
Contracted base CAGR moved ~8%→~9% with Q1 projects; called 'still pretty conservative'; reaffirmed 10%+
JSD models ~13% EBITDA CAGR; argues EPS CAGR 'north of 20%' on financial leverage

forward.note: 8%→9% contracted floor is mgmt breadcrumbing; Street/desk see beat by 200-300bps.

trail.val: $5.1bn sanctioned ≈ ~1.5 GW installed; ~3.5 GW incremental BTM could add ~$2.5bn annual EBITDA over time; 5 GW Siemens reserve
NEO (5th PI), 682MW, ~$2.3bn, 12.5-yr, ISD H2-2028, 5x build multiple
~15% upside if 2030 BTM targets hit; WMB discounting ~5 of its 8 GW BTM target; 9.5x power SOTP

forward.note: BTM 8 GW 2030 target; equity discounting only ~5 GW per WFC.

trail.val: Growth capex midpoint raised to $7.3bn for FY26
Power Express FID, 950 MMcf/d to Virginia
Power Express upsized to 750 MMcf/d, online 2030; Silver Spur (Idaho) 275 MMcf/d ISD early-2030

forward.note: Long-dated transmission EBITDA (run-rate only 2031-32) — long-cycle compounder.

trail.val: PI partnership/JV or 'creative financing' promised within 'a couple of months'; leverage 4.1x
Investor feedback on PI-JV 'lukewarm' — questions on why and low upfront multiple; sell-off risk if done in isolation

forward.note: Financing structure is the near-term swing; bears flag PI earnings-quality / terminal value.

trail.val: Woodside on track; first cargo ~2029; 1.5 MTPA offtake option
